channel.title: Administration on Pi Stack
channel.description: Recent content in Administration on Pi Stack

item.title: Cockpit vs Webmin vs Ajenti: Best Self-Hosted Server Management Web UI 2026
item.description: Managing Linux servers through SSH terminals works fine for a single machine. But once you are running five, ten, or fifty servers — or you manage servers for clients who refuse to use a command line — a web-based administration interface becomes essential.

item.title: PostfixAdmin vs Modoboa vs iRedAdmin: Self-Hosted Mail Server Admin Panels 2026
item.description: Managing a self-hosted email server without a web-based admin panel means logging into your server, running SQL queries to add domains, editing Postfix config files, and manually creating mailbox entries. For anyone running a production mail server serving multiple domains and users, this approach does not scale.
